# ADR-005: Devcontainer for CI/Local Parity

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

Previously, contributors had to manage local .NET SDK versions manually, leading to "works on my machine" failures and CI/local discrepancies. The CI pipeline also used a separate configuration from the local dev setup. This was addressed in a dedicated devcontainer contributor workflow ticket.

## Decision

Introduce a `.devcontainer/` configuration (Dockerfile + `devcontainer.json`) that:
- Pins the exact .NET SDK version
- Runs `dotnet restore` automatically on container creation
- Is used by **both** GitHub Actions CI and local contributors

The CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) uses the Dev Container CLI to spin up and exec inside the same devcontainer image, ensuring identical build and test environments.

## Consequences

- **Enables:** Reproducible builds locally and in CI with zero environment drift. Contributors using VS Code get automatic setup via "Reopen in Container".
- **Requires:** Docker to be available for both local development and CI runners.
- **Note:** `global.json` also pins the .NET SDK version (`6.0.428`) as a belt-and-suspenders measure for contributors who run `dotnet` directly without the devcontainer.
